chunk_summary: Choking prevention for under-twos emphasizes safe food forms, supervision, and education on high-risk foods and first aid, tailored to developmental stages.
learning_objectives: Identify high-risk choking hazard foods for children under two years old.
Understand safe food preparation and feeding practices to reduce choking risks.
Learn the importance of supervision and proper seating during feeding.
Recognize the difference between gagging and choking and respond appropriately.
key_facts: Choking risk is highest from the start of complementary feeding and remains high through 4 years.
High-risk foods include whole nuts, popcorn, hard candies, raw apple chunks, and sausages cut into round discs.
Safe food forms include cooking and mashing hard fruits/vegetables, grating apples, and cutting round foods lengthwise.
Children should eat seated upright in a stable chair with supervision; eating while walking or lying down increases aspiration risk.
Caregivers should learn age-appropriate choking first aid, especially in households with grandparents providing care.
reference_quote: Choking risk peaks when complementary feeding begins and remains high through 4 years, so prevention should be taught as part of routine feeding counseling.
